title: zinowiewia micrantha
intro: Zinowiewia micrantha, also known as the Mexican buckeye, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Its medicinal properties come from its roots and stems, which contain alkaloids and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Zinowiewia micrantha include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the roots and stems are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to leverage the medicinal properties of this plant.
